info.title: Cryptlex Web API
info.description:
The Cryptlex Web API provides programmatic access to our services and adheres to the RESTful design principles. We strive for clear communication and ease of use for both experienced and novice developers.

The key features of the Web API are:
1. All requests must be made over HTTPS for secure communication.
2. All URLs are resource-oriented to help with navigating the API in a predictable manner.
3. Responses consistently use JSON format, even for error messages.
4. Standard HTTP status codes indicate success or failure, and detailed error messages help identify and resolve issues.

# Versioning
All API requests are versioned using the major version prefixed by a 'v' in the URL e.g. v1, v2, v3. The latest supported version is v3 (3.x.x).

We follow Semantic Versioning. Given a version number `MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH`, the version is incremented in the following manner:

* MAJOR version when we make incompatible/breaking API changes,
* MINOR version when we add functionality in a backward-compatible manner, and
* PATCH version when we make backward-compatible bug fixes.

When we introduce breaking changes to the API, we will release a new versioned URL endpoint. You can refer to our [changelog](https://docs.cryptlex.com/changelog/web-api) for changes made till date.

# Errors
The API uses standard HTTP status codes to indicate the success or failure of the API call. All errors contain 4xx or 5xx status codes in the reponse header. The body of the response will contain the error message in the following format:
```
{
    "message" : "Email address is already taken!",
    "code"    : "DUPLICATE_FIELD_VALUE"  // conditional
}
```
# Rate Limiting
A single IP address can make a maximum burst of 50 requests per 5 second window, regardless of authentication. If we observe patterns of abuse, the IP address may be temporarily or permanently blacklisted.

Rate limit status can be checked in the returned HTTP headers of any API request:

| Header                 | Description   |
| -----------------------|---------------|
| X-Rate-Limit-Limit     | The the rate limit period (eg. 1m, 12h, 1d).                      |
| X-Rate-Limit-Remaining | The number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window.|
| X-Rate-Limit-Reset     | The UTC date time when the limits reset.                          |

tags.name: Accounts
tags.description: Account management helps you manage your account related details. It includes API endpoints for updating account details, changing account status, changing account plan etc.

tags.name: ActivationLogs
tags.description:
Activation logs provide a complete history of all the license activations and deactivations.

They can be used for auditing the license usage over time.

tags.name: Activations
tags.description:
A license activation is said to occur when your customer uses a license key to activate the license in your application on his/her machine.

By default all activations are node-locked, it means that the machine fingerprint is stored along with the activation details.

tags.name: Analytics
tags.description: You can gain important insights regarding how your customers are using your products and track key metrics like activations over time, activations by country, activations by OS and much more.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
This documentation outlines the multiple ways for obtaining an access token (AT) for authenticating your API requests with Cryptlex. You may obtain an access token by using various authentication methods like Email-Password, SAML SSO, and OIDC2.

For prolonged sessions on a server-side application, you can use personal access tokens (PAT), which can be managed on the [Admin Portal](https://app.cryptlex.com/api/personal-access-tokens). Here are a few guidelines to follow with personal access tokens:

1. Never share your PATs in publicly accessible areas, such as GitHub, client-side code, etc. 
2. Consider rotating your PATs regularly to enhance security.
3. Grant minimal permissions to each PAT based on its specific purpose.
4. Utilize environment variables or secure storage mechanisms to safeguard your PATs within your code.

tags.name: AutomatedEmailEventLogs
tags.description: Automated email event logs provide a complete history of all the automated emails triggered in last 15 days.

tags.name: AutomatedEmails
tags.description: Automated emails can be used to send emails automatically to your customers when events like "license.created", "license.renewed" etc. occur.

tags.name: EntitlementSets
tags.description: Entitlement sets group multiple feature entitlements, allowing you to manage and assign them collectively.

tags.name: EventLogs
tags.description: Every resource which is created, updated or deleted is logged along with the user who did that operation. This provides you a complete audit log of every activity going on in your account.

tags.name: FeatureFlags
tags.description: Feature flags help you create different versions of your product with different features.

tags.name: Features
tags.description: Features allow you to define specific functionalities of your product that can be granted as entitlements.

tags.name: Licenses
tags.description:
A license is what you use to license your application. Licenses are grouped under products and inherit all the properties of license policy attached to the product.

You can override all the properties of the license inherited from a license policy if needed.

tags.name: LicenseTemplates
tags.description:
A license template acts as a blueprint for the licenses you create for your customers and helps you implement different types of licensing models.

A single license template can be shared across different products and each product must have a default license template.

tags.name: MaintenancePolicies
tags.description: A maintenance policy can be used to configure how the end-user can update the software (product). A maintenance policy can be used to allow free updates, minor version updates, major version updates upto a specific duration etc.

tags.name: OrganizationActivations
tags.description: Organization Activations endpoints allow organization users with the 'organization-admin' role to manage activations within the organization.

tags.name: OrganizationLicenses
tags.description: Organization Licenses endpoints allow organization users with the 'organization-admin' role to manage licenses within the organization.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: An organization is a group of Users with the `organization-admin` and `user` role which can be assigned to a License. This allows you to assign a License to a group of Users, and allows you to delegate the management of Users to an `organization-admin`.

tags.name: OrganizationUserGroups
tags.description: An Organization User Group represents a group of Users in an Organization. It allows Users in the organization with the `organization-admin` role to manage groups of users that can be assigned to licenses.

tags.name: OrganizationUsers
tags.description: Organization Users endpoints allow organization users with the 'organization-admin' role to manage users within the organization.

tags.name: PersonalAccessTokens
tags.description:
Personal access tokens are the preferred way for third party applications and scripts to authenticate with the Cryptlex Web API. 

They function similar to API keys and offer fine grained access control. For example in order to create licenses from a script you can create a personal access token with scope (permission) `license:write`. 

You should always scope the token access to minimum required resources, by adding minimum number of permissions.

tags.name: Products
tags.description: A product refers to your application which you want to license. You will typically create a single product for all your application versions.

tags.name: ProductVersions
tags.description:
Product versions can be used to create different versions of your application with the help of feature flags.

You will typically create a single product for all your application versions and use product versions for different versions of your application. For example, if you offer two versions of your app PRO and LITE then you can create two product versions.

tags.name: ReleaseChannels
tags.description: A release channel refers to the channel (e.g. stable, beta etc.) for which the release is being created.

tags.name: ReleaseFiles
tags.description: A release file contains the information of the uploaded file including url, checksum, size etc.

tags.name: ReleasePlatforms
tags.description: A release platform refers to the OS for which the release is being created.

tags.name: Releases
tags.description: Release management helps you in protection of your software applications from unlicensed distribution. It allows you to host software applications of any size and type on our servers or your own servers, implement auto updates in your application and much more.

tags.name: ResellerActivations
tags.description: Reseller Activations endpoints allow reseller users with the 'reseller-admin' role to manage activations within the reseller.

tags.name: ResellerLicenses
tags.description: Reseller Licenses endpoints allow reseller users with the 'reseller-admin' role to manage licenses within the reseller.

tags.name: ResellerOrganizations
tags.description: Reseller Organizations endpoints allow reseller users with the 'reseller-admin' role to manage organizations within the reseller.

tags.name: Resellers
tags.description: The Reseller feature empowers you to entrust third parties or partners with the responsibility of distributing and managing licenses and users seamlessly on your behalf.

tags.name: ResellerUsers
tags.description: Reseller Users endpoints allow reseller users with the 'reseller-admin' role to manage users within the reseller.

tags.name: Roles
tags.description:
Role management helps you manage authorization, which enables you to specify the resources that users in your application are allowed to access. Role management lets you treat groups of users as a unit by assigning users to roles such as manager, sales, developer, and so on.

Role claims refer to permissions which you assign to a role. Every resource has read and write permission which you can add to a role.

tags.name: Tags
tags.description: Tags help you to group licenses across or within products. You can search licenses using tags.

tags.name: TrialActivations
tags.description:
A trial activation is said to occur when your customer activates the trial in your application on his/her machine.

By default all trial activations are node-locked, it means that the machine fingerprint is stored along with the trial activation details.

tags.name: TrialPolicies
tags.description:
A trial policy is similar to a license policy, with the difference that it is applicable for trials only. You can create a trial policy using license policies too, but this offers you a cheap and convenient way of doing so.

A single trial policy can be shared across different products and each product may have a trial policy. Not attaching a trial policy to a product disables trials automatically.

tags.name: Users
tags.description:
A user refers to your customer whom you want to license your product. 

Cryptlex provides identity management for your customers, which means you can authenticate your customers using email/password, and other features like password reset etc.

tags.name: WebhookEventLogs
tags.description: Webhook event logs provide a complete history of all the webhook events triggered in last 15 days.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Using webhooks you can subscribe to events, that happen within your Cryptlex account. When one of those events is triggered, we'll send an HTTP POST payload to the webhook's configured URL.
